signals.raw: Based on the search results from April 15, 2026, here are the key developments:

signals.headline: Ceasefire Expires April 21st Without Major Breakthrough | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Ceasefire Expires April 21st Without Major Breakthrough | YES | HIGH confidence**

The temporary two-week ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran will expire on Monday, April 21st[1]. First-round negotiations ended without a deal, with major disagreements remaining over nuclear commitments and Strait of Hormuz control[1].
matched_markets: Will the US x Iran ceasefire be extended by April 21, 2026?
US x Iran permanent peace deal by April 22, 2026?
US x Iran diplomatic meeting by April 18, 2026?

signals.headline: Israel-Lebanon Talks Commence | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Israel-Lebanon Talks Commence | YES | HIGH confidence**

Secretary of State Marco Rubio is hosting the first direct high-level talks between Israel and Lebanon[4]. This marks unprecedented direct contact between the two sides, though violence continues on the ground with Israeli strikes persisting in southern Lebanon[3].

signals.headline: Negotiations Stalled with Iran | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Negotiations Stalled with Iran | YES | HIGH confidence**

U.S. negotiators left Islamabad with virtually no progress after 21 hours of talks[3]. Vice President JD Vance indicated Iran refused to accept American terms on nuclear development prevention[3].
